title: Callicarpa subternata
taxonomy: Callicarpa subternata is classified within the family Lamiaceae, which includes plants commonly known as mint or deadnettles. The genus Callicarpa is typically grouped within the subfamily Lamioideae. This species is native to Southeast Asia, and its classification helps to understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the region. Further classification of Callicarpa subternata may include its specific rank within the Lamioideae subfamily and the Lamiaceae family. The taxonomic classification of Callicarpa subternata continues to be studied and refined as new information becomes available.

intro: Callicarpa subternata, also known as Purple Fruit Callicarpa, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Callicarpa subternata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
